headline: East Malaysia SPM Scores Fall 15 Points Below National
context: SPM 2025 results showed Sabah and Sarawak students averaged 15 points below Peninsula counterparts in core subjects.

cards.big: East Malaysian SPM students scored 15 points below Peninsula in 2025
cards.sub: Gap widened from 11 points in 2022 despite federal equalization programmes

cards.big: Mathematics pass rate in rural Sabah was 52% compared to 78% nationally
cards.sub: Teacher vacancies in STEM hit 35% in East Malaysia versus 12% Peninsula

cards.big: Only 18% of Sarawak SPM candidates achieved at least 3 A grades in 2025
cards.sub: National average was 31% with Selangor leading at 38% of candidates

cards.big: English language proficiency gap was 22 percentage points between regions
cards.sub: Rural schools average only 3 hours English weekly versus 6 hours nationally

cards.big: Score gaps test whether education quality follows geography more than policy
SPM scores 15 points below national average — East Malaysia's education gap starts before the exam.
